sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Larry, is a 50-year-old male who has been experiencing chronic back pain for the past couple of years.
He reports a sharp stabbing pain in his back that radiates down to his right leg, specifically to his big toe.
He also experiences numbness and tingling in his right leg, especially to the foot.
He denies any loss of sensation in the genital or rectal area and any weakness.
He has tried physical therapy, strong pain medications, and was even considered for surgery in the past but would like to avoid it.
He has gained weight due to his limited mobility and has had to give up his recent hobby of playing golf.
He has no family history of spine conditions and is a non-smoker, having quit 20 years ago.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has no overlying erythema or ecchymosis on the musculoskeletal back exam.
There is midline tenderness at L4-L5 disk space with right-sided lumbar paravertebral tenderness.
The patient experiences pain with lumbar flexion and supine straight leg test is positive.
On neurological examination, there is decreased sensation to light touch at the right lateral thigh at L4 dermatome.
Patellar and achilles reflexes are symmetrical.
Test Results: The patient's MRI shows disk herniation with nerve impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are consistent with an L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy.
Problem list: Lumbar back pain; L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy; Radiculopathy; Chronic back pain.
Plan:
I prescribed a spinal injection with a corticosteroid, which will be administered under fluoroscopy to help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
I will monitor the patient's response to the injection and schedule follow-up appointments as needed.
The patient will have the option to have up to four injections per year if needed.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a follow-up appointment to assess the effectiveness of the injection and to discuss further treatment options if necessary.
